title: These are Boston’s highest-paid tech CEOs, BBJ data shows
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Wayfair CEO Niraj Shah led Boston's tech CEO compensation in 2025 with $280.9M in stock and cash.
Vincent Roche of Analog Devices and Neil Barua of PTC Inc. followed with compensations of $27.5M and $24.3M.
The Boston Business Journal analyzed the top 10 CEO compensations using SEC proxy statements including stock and bonuses.

title: Karen Read’s SUV heads to auction in Woburn. Here’s what readers think it’s worth.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 2021 Lexus LX 570 linked to Karen Read will be auctioned in Woburn on May 14.
Auction respondents estimate the SUV could sell between less than $70,000 to over $500,000.
The SUV had issues like a dead battery and missing taillight but was refurbished before auction.

title: Shooting on Memorial Drive in Cambridge leaves ‘at least’ one wounded, police say
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: At least one person was wounded in a police-involved shooting on Memorial Drive in Cambridge.
The suspect is in custody, and authorities report no ongoing threat to the public.
Cambridge police advised residents to avoid the area, citing a heavy police presence near River Street.

title: Police investigating viral video of apparent food contamination at McDonald’s in Mass.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Southbridge police investigate a viral video of a McDonald's employee allegedly contaminating food by eating fries before repackaging them.
The Southbridge McDonald's franchise owners have terminated the involved employees and are collaborating with authorities to address the incident.
Local health officials found no violations, and the restaurant emphasizes safety measures and cooperation with ongoing investigations.

title: MassINC poll: Many Bay Staters say their quality of life is slipping in Mass.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: MassINC poll shows 42% of Massachusetts residents feel worse off financially compared to last year.
Poll reveals 65% of residents ages 35-44 find housing unaffordable, with broader economic concerns in healthcare and taxes.
Only 27% of surveyed residents expect financial improvement next year, highlighting deepening economic pessimism in Massachusetts.

title: Celtics reportedly tried to land Giannis Antetokounmpo at the trade deadline
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics pursued Giannis Antetokounmpo at the trade deadline along with Lakers, Knicks, Timberwolves, and Cavaliers.
Celtics face strategic changes after another playoff collapse, President Brad Stevens emphasizes adding paint scoring.
Bucks open to trading Antetokounmpo, who has two years left on his contract, for significant assets.

title: Student charged in Danvers home invasion murder appears in court
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, 18, charged with murder and home invasion, pleads not guilty in Essex Superior Court.
DeMayo, accused of killing Janet Swallow, 68, attends arraignment remotely from Bridgewater State Hospital.
Judge grants request for independent competency evaluation; next status hearing set for May 29.

title: Mass. Senate passes bill to protect immigrants, with notable differences from House version
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts Senate passed an immigrant protection bill, differing significantly from the House version passed earlier in March.
Senate bill bans warrantless civil immigration arrests at sensitive locations, unlike the narrower House restrictions.
House bill allows some 287(g) agreements and includes policies for correctional facilities, differing from the Senate's provisions.

title: Kevin Garnett, Paul Pierce analyze what went wrong with ‘robotic’ Celtics offense
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kevin Garnett and Paul Pierce criticized the Celtics' reliance on the 3-point shot, noting a lack of offensive adjustments.
The Celtics have blown significant playoff leads in recent seasons, including a 3-1 lead to the 76ers this year.
Garnett and Pierce highlighted personnel changes, including the loss of Kristaps Porzingis, affecting Boston's paint presence.

title: Waiting in line in Boston is brutal. Some spots are still worth it.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Brookline Lunch in Cambridge draws long lines for its popular baklava pancakes, praised by both locals and celebrities.
Cannonball Cafe in South Boston attracts crowds with its good coffee, friendly atmosphere, and strong online presence.
Modern Pastry and Neptune Oyster in the North End have lines due to their tradition, nostalgia, and renowned food offerings.
